title: Writing a Research Title - Comprehensive Quiz

questions.question: What is the most important element that defines the research problem?
Which part of the research is usually read first and is the most read part?
A research title contains the least words enough to describe the contents and the ______ of your research paper.
A research title can be revised any and many times as the research develops and reaches its final phase. It becomes final on its:
options: Final defence before the panel of judges

questions.question: A research title should be ______ rather than entertaining.
Which factor in choosing a research topic involves being interested because you have experienced it?
When deciding on a topic, it is important that sources of information are available. This refers to:
A topic that is significant to the community demonstrates:
Which factor limits a topic to what the teacher suggests, such as focusing only on the COVID-19 pandemic?
Personal resources in choosing a topic include considering if you can finish the research in terms of your _____ and financial physical capabilities.
Which of the following topics should be avoided in research?
Highly opinionated topics fall under which type of topic to avoid?
A topic that requires expertise should be avoided if you don't have enough knowledge about it. This is a:
Unavailability of reading materials and materials that are not updated make the subject:
The remedy for a topic that is too broad is to:
Some subjects are too narrow that extensive and thorough reading are required. This describes:
Titles that start with indefinite adjectives such as 'several,' 'many,' and 'some' make the topic:
Controversial topics are _____ opinionated topics that should be avoided in research.
According to the examples, which area of research includes magazines, journals, social media, books, and newspapers as sources?
The topic 'Cyberbullying and coping mechanisms among senior high school students' falls under which possible area of research?
The topic 'SONA as information campaign of the President's programs and projects' falls under which possible area of research?
The topic 'Functionality of social media among students during the pandemic' falls under which possible area of research?
The topic 'Inadequacies of private schools in the K to 12 implementations' falls under which possible area of research?
The topic 'The fallacy about COVID-19 vaccines' falls under which possible area of research?
One way to narrow down a topic is by exploring and extending the explanation of a:
One way to narrow down a topic is to talk over ideas with people who know:
One way to narrow down a topic is to focus on a specific:
When narrowing a topic, you should define the aim or desired _____ of the study.
Which type of study is exploratory, explanatory, or descriptive? This is part of defining the aim or outcome of the study.
Which of the following is an important question to ask when choosing a research topic?
options: Is my research useful to me, to my school, to my family, and to my community?

questions.question: A research study must be significant enough to make it worth your efforts as the researcher. This emphasizes the importance of:
The general concept 'Early Pregnancy' when narrowed down to 'Prevention of Early Pregnancy among Senior High School Students' is an example of:
A research title _____ the main thought or idea of the whole research paper.
A research title reflects the ______ under study.
A research title is expressed in few words possible and just enough to describe the:
A research title contains: What (subject matter), Where (place or locale), and _____ (participants or respondents).
In the title 'Study Habits of Senior High School Students in Public Schools of Bukidnon,' the 'what' is:
In the title 'The Correlation Between Later School Start Times and High School Athletes' Athletic Performances,' which is the independent variable?
In the title 'The Correlation Between Later School Start Times and High School Athletes' Athletic Performances,' which is the dependent variable?
In the title 'The Relationship Between Social Media and the Affective and Cognitive Behaviors of Grade 12 STEM Students,' the independent variable is:
